proof_lane.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_clean_overlay_proof_orchestration_contract CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test clean_overlay_proof_orchestration_contract -- --nocapture
proof_lane.covers: Clean-overlay proof-orchestration A4 operator runbook, when-to-use prerequisites, reservation expectations, RCH heartbeat-fresh/progress-stale cancellation guidance, peer-dirty blocker receipts, non-destructive cleanup/rollback, Agent Mail and br comment handoff templates, README/AGENTS markers, proof manifest/status rows, and no-claim boundaries stay aligned with the A1-A3 lane surfaces.
proof_lane.explicit_not_covered: Does not prove runtime correctness, broad workspace health, release readiness, performance improvement, live RCH fleet availability, permission to delete files or clean worktrees, or local Cargo fallback approval.

when_to_use: You are on main only and need to prove a focused slice without syncing peer dirt.
You hold an Agent Mail reservation for every path you intend to overlay.
RCH is reachable; you will never fall back to a local Cargo build.

peer_dirty_blocker.blocked_marker: # BLOCKED: clean-overlay refused; no RCH proof command emitted
peer_dirty_blocker.report_only_marker: # REPORT-ONLY: clean-overlay dry run; no RCH proof command emitted

non_destructive_cleanup.rollback_action: leave peer dirt unstaged; re-run overlay-scoped command after owned edits settle

bead_closeout_checklist: Overlay scoped to owned, reserved paths; peer dirt excluded, not synced.
Proof receipt (admitted command or blocker line) captured in bead and Agent Mail thread.
No branch/worktree/clone/deletion performed; no local Cargo fallback cited.
Reservations renewed (if continuing) or released (if done).
Claim cites only this lane's exact guarantee and no-claim boundaries.
no_claim_boundaries: No runtime-correctness claim outside the cited clean-overlay planner, blocker, and overlay-command surfaces.
No permission to delete files, clean worktrees, create branches, or create worktrees.
